summary:
The Agent concluded based on general demographic trends rather than specific textual evidence that as per the 2011 Indian census, there might be a slight difference between male and female life expectancies but did not provide concrete figures which are required directly addressing both genders' expected lifespan explicitly detailed within this context or available through referenced materials alone. To precisely answer such questions requiring definitive statistical outputs necessitates consulting official health records databases specifically related to censuses conducted during specified periods indicated clearly enough so they can serve as authoritative sources upon verification against other reliable datasets too when necessary before finalizing any conclusions drawn around these vital public health indicators impacting millions across diverse populations worldwide over time significantly influencing healthcare planning strategies among governments everywhere aiming towards improving overall quality living standards sustainably moving forward into future generations ahead effectively managing resources efficiently ensuring equitable access care services universally accessible promoting wellness longevity amongst all segments society regardless socioeconomic status cultural background religious beliefs etc.. \boxed{\text{(Males likely have higher LEb compared to Females)}} 

The summary focuses primarily on identifying gender differences implied indirectly via contextual clues while acknowledging limitations inherent absence explicit quantitative measures stipulated
